bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid by holding firm on the best value for every issue. The opponent’s opening offer gives me very low utility and provides no evidence of concessions yet, so I should counter with a strong anchor using only valid domain values.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still the beginning of the negotiation at t=0.02, and the opponent has not made meaningful concessions toward my preferences. Their latest offer is still very low utility for me and even worsens issueD compared with their opening offer, so I will only make a small concession while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.04 and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession; their offers remain clustered around values that are poor for me. I avoid repeating my exact previous bid while making only a small concession on issueC, preserving my strongest positions on issueA, issueB, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.06 and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded, repeatedly offering values that are very low for me. I will make only a small concession from my prior bids by varying issueC while holding firm on my highest-priority issueA and strong values on issueB/issueD.

I targeted a high-utility bid around 0.87 because it is still very early and the opponent’s offers remain extremely low for me with no meaningful concession on issueA, issueB, or issueC. Since their only variation has been on issueD, I am conceding there first while holding firm on my higher-value issues and avoiding repeating my last bid.

At t=0.10 it is still early, and the opponent has shown no meaningful concession, repeatedly offering valueF on almost every issue, which is very low utility for me. I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a modest concession on issueB, since my strongest priority is holding issueA at valueA and preserving strong outcomes on issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.856 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent has not shown meaningful concession, repeatedly offering valueF/valueE combinations that are very low for me. I will keep firm on my most important issueA and other high-value positions, while making a modest concession on issueC compared with my maximum bid to avoid simply repeating my last offer.

At t=0.14 it is still early, and the opponent has repeatedly offered near-minimum-value bids for me with no meaningful concession pattern. I am targeting a high-utility bid while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous offer, making only a modest concession from my ideal package to test flexibility without rewarding their firm low offers.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.16 and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded, repeatedly offering mostly valueF with only issueD alternating between valueE/valueF. I will hold firm on high-importance issueA and preserve strong values on issueB and issueC, while making a modest concession on the lower-priority issueD to avoid simply repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.73, since it is early at t=0.18 and the opponent has shown almost no meaningful concession, repeatedly offering valueF on issueA/B/C and only alternating issueD between valueE/valueF. I will not concede on high-importance issueA, but I can offer concessions on issueC and issueD where prior bids suggest possible opponent interest, while avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.74 for me, since we are still early at t=0.20 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent has held firm on valueF for issueA/issueB/issueC and alternates only issueD between valueE and valueF, so I will concede on issueD while protecting my higher-value issues, especially issueA.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid because it is early (t=0.22) and the opponent has not meaningfully conceded, repeatedly offering variants with valueF on issues A/B/C that are very poor for me. I concede mainly on lower-cost areas by moving issueD to valueF and issueC to valueD, while holding firm on issueA and issueB where giving up to their apparent preferred values would be expensive.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.71 because it is early-mid negotiation time (t=0.24), so there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent has held almost entirely to valueF on issues A/B/C and valueE/F on D, so I will keep my high-weight issueA and issueB strong while making concessions on lower-weight issueC and issueD, avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

At t=0.26 I am still targeting a solid positive-utility bid while making visible concessions on lower-priority dimensions rather than giving up issueA or issueB. The opponent has held firm at issueA/B/C=valueF and only alternates issueD between valueE/valueF, so I treat their messages/history as low-concession behavior and concede mainly on issueD while keeping my most important issueA at valueA.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 because it is only t=0.28 and the opponent has shown almost no real concession, repeatedly offering issueA/B/C as valueF and only alternating issueD between valueE/valueF. Since their behavior suggests they may care about issueD, I concede issueD to valueE while holding firm on my most important issues issueA and issueB, and I avoid repeating my immediately previous bid.

I targeted a still-strong utility of about 0.696, conceding on issueD to valueF, which the opponent has repeatedly offered, and making a modest concession on issueB while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueC. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure to move beyond maximal bids, but the opponent has shown almost no concession pattern, so I am not making a large drop yet. There are no opponent messages to weigh, and their offers remain clustered around valueF/valueE, which are very low for me.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69 because we are only at t=0.32, so there is time to hold firm on my highest-weight issueA while making visible concessions elsewhere. The opponent has been extremely rigid, repeatedly offering valueF on issueA/B/C and valueE/F on issueD, so I am conceding issueD to valueF and softening issueB while preserving high value on issueA and issueC. I am targeting a moderate utility bid around the mid-game rather than accepting the opponent's very low-utility pattern, since t=0.34 still leaves time. I am targeting a moderate-high utility bid around 0.67 while avoiding repeating my last exact offer. The opponent has stayed essentially fixed on issueA/B/C=valueF and issueD=valueE/F, so at t=0.38 I will concede mainly on lower-cost dimensions like issueD and some issueB movement while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueC.

At t=0.40 I am targeting a moderate utility around the mid-0.6 range: enough concession to explore agreement, but not so low that I reward the opponent’s complete lack of movement. I am targeting a moderate concession around 0.61 utility for me: still well above the opponent’s repeated very low offers, but more accommodating than my earlier bids. The opponent has not meaningfully conceded and keeps offering mostly all valueF with issueD alternating between valueE/valueF, so at t=0.44 I should test a bid close to their pattern without giving up my most important issue yet.

The opponent has held almost completely firm on issueA/issueB/issueC as valueF and alternates only on issueD between valueE/valueF, so I am matching their latest pattern on B/C/D while still protecting my most important issueA.

At t=0.48, I am targeting a moderate concession while preserving issueA, my highest-weight issue, at its best value. I am targeting about 0.375 utility by conceding issueB, issueC, and issueD to values the opponent has consistently signaled, while only partially conceding my most important issueA from valueA to valueB. At t=0.50 there is real mid-game pressure to move beyond my prior offers, and the opponent’s offers show no meaningful concession beyond alternating issueD between valueE/valueF, with no messages to modify that read.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.405, keeping a concession on issueB and issueC while preserving some value on the high-weight issueA. At t=0.52 there is time pressure but not enough to capitulate, especially since the opponent has not meaningfully conceded and keeps repeating very low-utility bids for me. Their offers consistently signal a preference for valueF on issueA/B/C and valueE or valueF on issueD, so I will keep concessions there but choose issueD valueF since it is valid and better for me.

I am targeting a moderate concession around 0.375 utility for me, keeping some value on the highest-weight issueA while conceding issueB and issueC fully and matching the opponent’s common issueD=valueE. At t=0.54 there is pressure to move, but not enough to accept their near-minimum repeated offers; their bids show they are holding firm around issueA/B/C=valueF and issueD=valueE/F with no informative messages.

I am targeting a still-positive concession around utility 0.405, preserving some value on the high-weight issueA while giving the opponent the F values they have consistently demanded on issues B and C. Time is past the midpoint, so I am conceding substantially, but the opponent has shown no real movement beyond alternating issueD between valueE and valueF, so I will choose the issueD value they have also offered that is better for me. At t=0.66 the opponent has been almost completely firm around issueA/B/C=valueF and only varies issueD between valueE/valueF, so their offers give me very low utility and show little concession. At t=0.68, the opponent has shown almost no concession, consistently insisting on issueA/B/C = valueF and alternating issueD between valueE and valueF.
